steps.action: Run runtime inventory workflow/valuation contracts and verify engine size + facade cleanup.
steps.expected: Workflow engine remains under 500 LOC with runtime dispatch behaviors preserved; redundant workflow facade removed.
steps.observed: `TS_InventoryDispatchStateRuntimeCoverageTest` (6 tests) and `TS_RuntimeInventoryValuationExecutableCoverageTest` passed in the targeted run; `FinishedGoodsWorkflowEngineService` line count is 459; no `FinishedGoodsWorkflowService` class found.

evidence.output: Workflow/runtime subset executed with passing coverage for TS_InventoryDispatchStateRuntimeCoverageTest, TS_RuntimeInventoryValuationExecutableCoverageTest, InventoryAdjustmentControllerTest, RawMaterialControllerTest, RawMaterialStockConstraintTest; engine file 459 LOC

assertions.title: Positive stock adjustments and RM adjustments

steps.action: Validate FG RECOUNT_UP and RM adjustment idempotency/accounting behavior from targeted tests and prior validated suite.
steps.expected: FG positive adjustments increase stock with journal postings; RM adjustment endpoint supports stock change and idempotent journal-backed posting.
steps.observed: `InventoryAdjustmentCostingMethodTest` contains explicit `createAdjustment_recountUpIncreasesStockAndDebitsInventory`; `RawMaterialAdjustmentServiceTest` covers RM adjustment increase/decrease, journal posting, and idempotent replay. Current rerun of integration-heavy tests is blocked by known bean-name collision, but earlier milestone evidence and focused runtime/controller tests remain green.

evidence.output: Controller/runtime inventory tests pass, but assertion-level integration evidence is blocked in this run because `InventoryAdjustmentCostingMethodTest` and `RawMaterialAdjustmentServiceTest` fail ApplicationContext startup from `inventoryValuationService` bean-name conflict

assertions.title: Batch expiry monitoring and negative stock prevention

steps.action: Run inventory controller/domain/runtime subset and verify expiry + negative-stock guard coverage.
steps.expected: Expiring-soon endpoint/query semantics and negative-stock guard behavior are enforced.
steps.observed: `RawMaterialStockConstraintTest` passed (`setCurrentStock_rejectsNegativeValues`); `RawMaterialControllerTest` passed (`expiringSoonBatches_clampsNegativeDaysToZero`); `InventoryBatchQueryServiceTest` contains combined sorted expiry-window coverage but is currently context-blocked by known bean conflict.

evidence.output: RawMaterialStockConstraintTest and RawMaterialControllerTest passed, but integration expiry-query confirmation (`InventoryBatchQueryServiceTest`) is blocked by the same `inventoryValuationService` bean-definition conflict

frictions.description: Inventory integration tests requiring full Spring context fail due duplicate bean name `inventoryValuationService` between reports and inventory modules.
frictions.resolution: Not resolved in this validation run; used passing runtime/controller/domain subset and previously collected evidence while recording blocker explicitly.

blockers.description: `ConflictingBeanDefinitionException`: bean name `inventoryValuationService` conflicts between `com.bigbrightpaints.erp.modules.reports.service.InventoryValuationService` and `com.bigbrightpaints.erp.modules.inventory.service.InventoryValuationService`, preventing Spring context startup for integration classes (`InventoryAdjustmentCostingMethodTest`, `RawMaterialAdjustmentServiceTest`, `InventoryBatchQueryServiceTest`).
blockers.impact: Blocks re-execution of inventory integration classes in this run; does not affect already-passing unit/runtime/contract subset used for validation evidence.

summary: Validated inventory hardening surface with mixed outcomes: VAL-INV-001 passed on direct runtime evidence, while VAL-INV-002 and VAL-INV-003 are blocked in this run because required integration tests cannot bootstrap due to duplicate `inventoryValuationService` bean naming conflict.
